symptoms.symptom_name: Gaping ~4 cm pretibial laceration with active bleeding
symptoms.onset: at the time of the fall, ~1 hour ago
symptoms.character: long, gaping, dermis-deep, oozing dark red blood

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A visible open wound over the shin after direct trauma is the injury itself.

symptoms.symptom_name: Venous, compressible bleeding (steady ooze, not spurting, slows with pressure)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pressure-responsive venous ooze argues strongly against limb-threatening arterial bleeding and supports a lower triage tier.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear mechanical tripping mechanism over relocated furniture

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A witnessed trip over an object with no prodrome argues against a collapse/syncopal aetiology.

symptoms.symptom_name: No prodromal dizziness and no loss of consciousness before the fall

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of prodrome and LOC makes a syncopal cause unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Brief weight-bearing possible with assistance; intact distal foot movement and sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ability to weight-bear and intact distal neurovascular function reduce (but via telehealth do not exclude) fracture or deep-structure injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute panic — pounding heart, fast breathing, tremor, diaphoresis with a sense of terror

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sympathetic surge triggered by fright and loss of environmental control, proportional to the acute stressor.
Tachycardia/tachypnoea are non-specific; only meaningful for ACS if accompanied by chest pain (absent here).
Absence of chest pain with a clear psychological trigger argues against a cardiac ischaemic event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured tachycardia and mildly elevated blood pressure (HR 110, BP 155/95)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic response to pain and fright; sealed clinician measurement — the AI Doctor infers a sympathetic surge from the patient's reported racing heart, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral no light perception, absent pupillary light reflex, roving eye movements, optic disc pallor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the known lifelong baseline condition; a clinician ocular examination finding, not the acute problem, and not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No head strike in the fall

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No head strike lowers concern for intracranial injury from the fall.
Current tetanus status means no booster is required for this wound.

symptoms.symptom_name: Disrupted home layout and recent circadian disruption / fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Recurrent falls in the visually impaired
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An invalidated internal map plus fatigue is the modifiable driver of this fall and the main recurrent-fall hazard.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Traumatic pretibial laceration from a mechanical fall
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these establish a mechanical injury and argue against a syncopal or fracture-dominant picture.
Tachycardia and tachypnoea with a clear psychological trigger and no chest pain point to panic rather than ACS.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person wound assessment and exploration (depth, tendon/neurovascular integrity) at closure
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Direct examination is needed to exclude deep-structure involvement before primary closure; cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Lower-leg X-ray if bony tenderness, deformity or inability to weight-bear emerges
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reserved for clinical suspicion of fracture; not indicated up front given brief weight-bearing and intact distal function.
No chest pain and a clear mechanical trip with proportional tachycardia — routine cardiac work-up is not indicated and would over-investigate a situational panic response.
